research_brief.title: Autonomous PDE Neural Operator Research
research_brief.question: Given the AI4Science PDE neural operator competition setting, autonomously discover relevant literature, repositories, datasets, checkpoints, baselines, and methods; then design, implement, execute, evaluate, and iteratively refine scientifically meaningful improvements.
research_brief.background: The current target problem is PDE neural operator research for short-window prediction under explicit physics participation constraints. The system should behave as an independent research engineer and scientific agent, not as a baseline-only tuner.
objectives: Autonomously retrieve and synthesize related work.
Autonomously discover runnable repos, data, and checkpoints.
Distinguish method innovation from engineering tuning.
Generate and test new hypotheses grounded in evidence.
Maintain research memory, program lineage, and reports.
constraints: The system must use physics information in training or inference when the task requires it.
The system should decide what assets and commands are needed instead of relying on hand-coded benchmark assumptions.
GPU preference is 6 and 7 when available.
Experiment duration should be chosen by the agent through commands and stopping rules, not by a fixed wall-clock cap.

secrets.purpose: OpenAI Agents SDK runtime and tool-using specialist agents.
Optional higher GitHub API rate limits for repository discovery.
